name: Stuffed Eggplant with Peanut Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons low sodium chicken broth, defatted
1 cup hot cooked rice (basmati or brown)
recipeInstructions:
1. Halve each eggplant and remove the tops. Scoop out the flesh with a melon baller, leaving shells about 1/4"-thick.
2. Chop the eggplant flesh and set aside.
3. Bring a pot of salted water to boil. Cook the shells for 2-3 minutes to soften, then invert and drain on paper towels.
4. In a microwave-safe casserole dish, combine onions, carrots, peppers, bok choy, broth, and chopped eggplant. Cover with vented plastic or lid and microwave on high power for 5 minutes, until peppers are softened.
5. Add snow peas, rice, soy sauce, hoisin sauce, peanut butter, ginger, and garlic. Mix well.
6. Arrange the eggplant shells in the casserole dish and cover. Microwave for an additional 2 minutes to heat through.
10 mins attention; 20 mins total
Per serving: 199 calories; 2.1 g total fat (9%)
